blocks.text: had committed. He was in this now.
had committed to what he could. He would not promise the rest.
had not said what he would do. He would know when he got there.

blocks.speaker: Nadia Reyes
blocks.content: What does that mean? For you. In a situation like this.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: The kind people don't usually think to hide because they don't know anyone is watching.

blocks.speaker: Nadia Reyes
blocks.content: And you think someone in our group did this.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: I think the door was locked from the inside and the window was twenty feet off the ground.

blocks.speaker: Nadia Reyes
blocks.content: Marcus was my closest friend. For ten years. I need to know what happened to him.
She held eye contact the entire time. The kind of steadiness that either means you have nothing to hide or everything to protect.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Then I hope the investigation gets there quickly.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: I think the evidence will tell us what happened. It always does. The only question is how long it takes.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: It's a certainty. I'm careful about those.

blocks.prompt: Nadia. After this conversation. What does Julian know?

options.label: She's grieving. This is what grief looks like when you're controlled.
She came to see what he knows. She's measuring his reach.
Both things are true at the same time.
